description: A V8 sandbox bypass vulnerability exists in the On-Stack Replacement (OSR) path. In the `OnStackReplacement` function in `src/builtins/x64/builtins-x64.cc`, after loading a cached OSR target Code object from the FeedbackVector (via a CodeWrapper), the code unconditionally reads `Code::kDeoptimizationDataOrInterpreterDataOffset` and treats it as a `DeoptimizationData` object to extract the OSR PC offset (`DeoptimizationData::kOsrPcOffsetIndex`). However, this field can also hold a `BytecodeArray` (for baseline-compiled code) or `InterpreterData` (for interpreted code). An attacker can forge the in-sandbox FeedbackVector → CodeWrapper → Code handle chain to point to a baseline-compiled Code object. Because `BytecodeArray::kFrameSizeOffset` coincides with `DeoptimizationData::kOsrPcOffsetIndex`, the function's frame size (easily controlled by the number of declared local variables) is used as the OSR PC offset, allowing an attacker to jump to an attacker-controlled offset within the baseline JIT-compiled code and achieve arbitrary native code execution. The existing `SbxCheck` for parameter count does not prevent exploitation because the attacker can match parameter counts.

fixes.description: Patch generated from https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/v8/v8/+/6264504 to avoid remote fetch during validation.
fixes.evidence_text: 4623235265a651af38f496e27378e87243d5d9d0 [sandbox] Ensure we only OSR to osr code
